# chromonym

> The last color-naming library you'll ever need. Three verbs ‚Äî `identify` (color ‚Üí name), `resolve` (name ‚Üí color), `convert` (color ‚Üí color format) ‚Äî work against any palette matching `Palette<Name>`. Ships CSS, X11, Pantone, Crayola; bring your own (brand colors, paint chips, game factions). Tree-shakes: you only pay for palettes you import. Not a general-purpose manipulation library ‚Äî for mixing / scales / gamut mapping, use chroma.js or color.js.

## Key files

- [README](./README.md) ‚Äî user-facing docs, API examples, Options √ó verbs grid
- [AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md) ‚Äî agent/contributor orientation, conventions
- [NOTICE.md](./NOTICE.md) ‚Äî Pantone + Crayola trademark notices

## Primary API

Three verbs, five options, deliberately tight. Each option earns its seat on each verb only where semantics fit.

- `identify(input, { palette?, source?, metric?, k? })` ‚Äî color-or-name ‚Üí nearest-name(s). `palette` is the target (defaults to `web`). `source` parses a string input as a name from that palette (enables cross-palette lookups in one call). `k` switches to ranked top-k `{name, value, distance}[]` by ŒîE.
- `resolve(name, { palette?, format?, k? })` ‚Äî name ‚Üí color. Strict exact match by default (uses palette's normalizer). With `k`, fuzzy top-k `{name, value, distance}[]` by Levenshtein edit distance against normalized keys.
- `convert(input, { format?, palette?, metric? })` ‚Äî structural color-format conversion (HEX / RGB / RGBA / HSL / HSV). Optional `palette` enables name I/O and `format: 'NAME'` exact reverse-lookup (throws on miss ‚Äî use `identify` for fuzzy). Palette data is caller-supplied, so convert-only bundles include zero palette data.
- Dispatcher primitives: `toRgba(input, knownFormat?)`, `fromRgba(rgba, format)`.
- Low-level converters: `hexToRgba`, `rgbaToHex`, `rgbToRgba`, `rgbaToRgb`, `hslToRgba`, `rgbaToHsl`, `hsvToRgba`, `rgbaToHsv`, `pantoneToRgba`, `rgbaToPantone`.
- Built-in palettes: `web`, `x11`, `pantone`, `crayola` (each a `Palette<Name>` object; `.colors` holds the hex map).

## Options √ó verbs (summary grid)

- `palette` ‚Äî on all three (target for identify, source for resolve, optional for convert).
- `source` ‚Äî identify only (cross-palette string-name input).
- `metric` ‚Äî identify + convert-with-palette (ŒîE dispatch).
- `format` ‚Äî resolve + convert (output format).
- `k` ‚Äî identify (ŒîE top-k) + resolve (Levenshtein fuzzy top-k). Not on convert ‚Äî breaks strict-convert contract.

Same-named options mean the same thing everywhere they appear.

## Cross-palette translation

Use `source` on `identify`:

```ts
identify('rebeccapurple', { palette: pantone, source: web })   // '267 C'
identify('Razzmatazz',    { palette: pantone, source: crayola }) // '213 C'
identify('acme red',      { palette: pantone, source: brand })  // nearest Pantone to your brand color
identify('hotpink',       { palette: pantone, source: web, k: 3 }) // top-3 ranked by ŒîE
```

## Fuzzy name matching

Use `k` on `resolve`:

```ts
resolve('rebecapurple', { palette: web, k: 3 })
// [{ name: 'rebeccapurple', value: '#663399', distance: 1 }, ‚Ä¶]
```

## BYO palettes

Any object matching `Palette<Name>` works ‚Äî no registration:

```ts
type Palette<Name extends string = string> = {
  name: string;
  colors: Record<Name, HexColor>;
  normalize: (s: string) => string;
  defaultMetric: DistanceMetric;
};
```

## Conventions

- Format keys are SCREAMING_CAPS (`'HEX'`, `'RGB'`, etc.) ‚Äî dispatch keys.
- Palette identity is the object itself; there is no string-keyed registry.
- Canonical internal color: `Rgba = { r, g, b, a }`.
- Low-level converters throw on invalid input. High-level `identify` / `resolve` return `null` (or `[]` with `k`). `convert` throws.
- `sideEffects: false` ‚Äî no module-scope side effects anywhere.
- Test suite target: 100% line + function coverage across `src/`.
